preamble: Reduction of velar nasals to palatal glides represents a weakening of the nasal articulation, often as a stage in the loss of nasal consonants or their transformation into glides in specific vocalic environments.

examples.note: In certain historical developments, the velar nasal weakened through a glide stage, leading to the loss of the nasal consonant and the formation of diphthongs or glides.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
